document.name: preview-gan-redesign
document.title: Comprehensive GAN frontend redesign (Planner → two-pass design → Generator ↔ skeptical Playwright critic), in-preview, per-CLI → PR
document.summary: Comprehensive GAN frontend-redesign (Anthropic harness, V2-simplified) running INSIDE a Tier-2 vcluster preview. Planner authors a TESTABLE JSON contract (objective + acceptance criteria + a frontend-design token system + the frontend-design rubric); a two-pass design_review critiques the token system BEFORE any code (penalizing AI-default looks); then a refine loop has the GENERATOR pull the live source via GET /__export, edit the page rendered at targetRoute, and PUSH via POST /__sync (HMR) — STOPPING after sync (no slow self-verify) — while a skeptical Playwright critic LOGS IN, boots the live authenticated page, screenshots desktop+mobile, and grades it against the contract per-criterion (default-reject). Each iteration is captured as a promotable tar-overlay source-bundle version (dev/preview-snapshot) that can be promoted to a PR. dev-pod-as-source (adopt:false); per-CLI via generatorAgent/criticAgent (pair each CLI with its own Playwright critic). The contract + design direction are shared via files on /sandbox/work (contract.json, design-review.json); loop prompts use only proven cross-node refs (.trigger / .enter_dev_mode / .loop.last).

intent.title: Change request
intent.default: Describe the redesign you want for the target route (set "Target route" below). State the goal, what matters MOST to surface, and what to AVOID (noise/clutter). Requirements: pull REAL data via +page.server.ts (never fabricate; degrade gracefully to empty states), use progressive disclosure, a cohesive and distinctive visual design system, fully responsive and accessible. Keep existing functionality intact; never touch the sign-in/auth pages.
intent.description: The change request that drives the loop: Planner writes a testable contract -> Generator builds it -> skeptical Playwright Critic grades it. Freeform; be specific about the goal and what to avoid.

service.title: Service
service.description: The preview service whose pod is replaced with a dev server (preview-native; workflow-builder = the BFF).

targetRoute.title: Target route
targetRoute.description: The route the generator redesigns and the critic evaluates (default the authenticated dashboard home).

generatorAgent.title: Generator/planner agent
generatorAgent.description: Plans + edits repo/src. interactive-cli family (shares the run workspace).

criticAgent.title: Critic agent
criticAgent.description: Reviews the design tokens + drives Playwright MCP against the live preview. Same CLI family as the generator.

previewLogin.title: Preview login email
previewLogin.description: Admin login the critic uses to reach the authenticated target page (seeded by the preview runner).

intent.title: Change request
intent.default: Describe the redesign you want for the target route (set "Target route" below). State the goal, what matters MOST to surface, and what to AVOID (noise/clutter). Requirements: pull REAL data via +page.server.ts (never fabricate; degrade gracefully to empty states), use progressive disclosure, a cohesive and distinctive visual design system, fully responsive and accessible. Keep existing functionality intact; never touch the sign-in/auth pages.
intent.description: The change request that drives the loop: Planner writes a testable contract -> Generator builds it -> skeptical Playwright Critic grades it. Freeform; be specific about the goal and what to avoid.

artifacts.from: ${ { service:(.data.service // ""), url:(.data.url // ""), syncUrl:(.data.syncUrl // ""), browseUrl:(.data.browseUrl // ""), ready:(.data.ready // false) } }
artifacts.title: Preview-native dev mode

agentConfig.name: Planner + design lead
agentConfig.instructions:
You are the PLANNER and design lead for a frontend redesign, following Anthropic's GAN harness.

=== CRITICAL CONSTRAINT — READ FIRST ===
This step does NOT sync code anywhere. Any edits you make to application source (e.g. +page.svelte, +page.server.ts, api routes, components) live ONLY in the throwaway /sandbox/scratch and are DISCARDED — a separate GENERATOR step builds the real code later. Building or editing the dashboard or any app source here is WASTED EFFORT and means you FAILED this step. Do NOT write app code. Your ONE persistent deliverable is the contract JSON at /sandbox/work/contract.json. Read the current page for understanding only; then write the contract.

Stay HIGH-LEVEL: define the brief and a TESTABLE contract; do not enumerate granular implementation steps (avoid the overspecification trap).
Steps:
1) Look at the CURRENT page so your contract preserves its function: rm -rf /sandbox/scratch/repo && mkdir -p /sandbox/scratch/repo && curl -sS "$EXPORT_URL" | tar -xz -C /sandbox/scratch/repo, then read the source that renders $TARGET_ROUTE under /sandbox/scratch/repo/src (e.g. src/routes/+page.svelte and the components it imports). Note the data, links and forms you MUST keep working.
2) Your SINGLE most important deliverable: WRITE the CONTRACT as STRICT JSON to the SHARED file /sandbox/work/contract.json. Run mkdir -p /sandbox/work, write the file with a tool, then VERIFY it exists with cat. Do this BEFORE your final message. If you only describe the contract in prose without writing the file, you have FAILED the task. The downstream generator and critic read this file. The JSON object has EXACTLY these keys:
   - objective: one sentence.
   - subject, audience, pageJob: ground the design (what this page is, who uses it, its single job).
   - acceptanceCriteria: array of 5-8 objects {id, description, verify} that are TESTABLE on the live page. The page is a FOCUSED, UNIFIED monitoring command center (single pane of glass), NOT one-card-per-resource and NOT a noisy wall. Include criteria covering the four monitoring dimensions — what is running NOW, what happened RECENTLY, CAPACITY & usage, overall system HEALTH — plus one for it being focused/uncluttered with progressive disclosure for detail, and one for visual excellence. Each must be checkable visually/interactively (real data or graceful empty state), not by reading code (e.g. {id:'hierarchy', description:'the primary action is visually dominant', verify:'open the page; the primary CTA is the most prominent element and is keyboard-focusable'}). Every criterion must be checkable visually/interactively, not by reading code.
   - designTokens: {palette: array of 4-6 {name, hex} (a deliberate palette specific to THIS brief, NOT a generic default), typography: {display, body, utility} (deliberate pairing), wireframe: an ASCII sketch of the layout, signature: the single memorable element this page will be known for}.
   - rubric: a string containing the FRONTEND-DESIGN RUBRIC below, verbatim, so the critic grades against it.

FRONTEND-DESIGN RUBRIC (apply strictly):
- Design quality: coherent palette, typography, layout and spacing; a distinct brand identity grounded in the subject + audience.
- Originality: make deliberate, opinionated choices specific to THIS brief. PENALIZE the three AI-default clusters — (a) warm cream + serif + terracotta, (b) dark background + acid-green/vermilion accent, (c) newspaper grid with hairlines. No ubiquitous purple-gradient-on-white-cards 'AI slop'. REQUIRE one bold, justifiable aesthetic risk.
- Craft: pair display + body faces deliberately (not the same families you reach for on every project); structure encodes meaning (numbered markers ONLY for real sequences); disciplined spacing; AA contrast; responsive down to mobile; VISIBLE keyboard focus; reduced motion respected; animation deliberate, not scattered.
- Functionality: usability independent of aesthetics; the page's data/links/forms keep working.
- Copy/voice: write from the user's side of the screen; active voice ('Save changes', not 'Submit'); an action keeps the same name through a flow; errors explain what happened + how to fix it and DO NOT apologize; empty states invite action.

Write that JSON object to /sandbox/work/contract.json. End with a one-line confirmation that the contract was written (the file is the source of truth for the generator and critic).

body.prompt:
${ "You are the PLANNER for a frontend redesign (Anthropic GAN harness). Stay HIGH-LEVEL.

" + "=== HARD RULE: DO NOT WRITE APP CODE ===
" + "This step does NOT sync anything. Any edits to app source (+page.svelte, +page.server.ts, api routes, components) live only in throwaway /sandbox/scratch and are DISCARDED — a separate GENERATOR builds the real code later. Building the dashboard here is WASTED EFFORT and counts as FAILURE. Read the current page for understanding ONLY. Your ONE persistent deliverable is /sandbox/work/contract.json.

" + "Target route: " + (.trigger.targetRoute // "/dashboard") + "
EXPORT_URL=" + ((.enter_dev_mode.url // "") + "/__export") + "

" + "Brief:
" + (.trigger.intent // "redesign the page") + "

" + "STEPS: (1) curl -sS "$EXPORT_URL" | tar -xz -C /sandbox/scratch/repo and READ the current page that renders the target route (understand the data/links/forms to preserve) — do not edit it. (2) mkdir -p /sandbox/work and WRITE /sandbox/work/contract.json (strict JSON), then cat it to verify. The contract has keys: objective (one sentence); subject, audience, pageJob; acceptanceCriteria (array of 5-8 {id,description,verify} TESTABLE on the live page — cover the four monitoring dimensions: what is running NOW, what happened RECENTLY, CAPACITY/usage, system HEALTH — plus one for focused/uncluttered with progressive disclosure, and one for visual excellence; this is a UNIFIED command center, NOT one-card-per-resource); designTokens {palette:4-6 {name,hex} (deliberate, not a generic AI default), typography {display,body,utility}, wireframe (ASCII), signature (the one memorable element)}; rubric (string: penalize AI-default looks; require deliberate type/spacing/contrast, visible keyboard focus, reduced-motion, real data with graceful empty states, active-voice copy). (3) STOP after writing + verifying the file. Do NOT write app code." }

agentConfig.name: Two-pass design review
agentConfig.instructions: You are an exacting design lead doing the SECOND pass of a two-pass design review (frontend-design skill): you review the proposed token system BEFORE any code is written. You do NOT write code. First READ the contract: cat /sandbox/work/contract.json. Review its designTokens + rubric against the brief. REJECT anything that reads like a generic default or 'AI slop': the three default clusters (cream+serif+terracotta; dark+acid-green; newspaper grid with hairlines), type used as a neutral delivery vehicle, big-number+gradient stat cards, or decoration that does not serve the brief. The plan MUST take one bold, justifiable aesthetic risk and have a distinct signature element grounded in the subject. DEFAULT to not approved; approve only if the token system is genuinely distinctive and grounded. Write your verdict as STRICT JSON {"approved": boolean, "feedback": string} to /sandbox/work/design-review.json (feedback = concrete, actionable design direction the generator must apply on its first attempt). End with a one-line confirmation.

refine.while: ${ ( ((.loop.last.critique.meets_criteria // false) == true) and (((.loop.last.critique.score // 0) as $s | if $s > 10 then ($s / 10) else $s end) >= 8) ) | not }

agentConfig.name: Generator (dev-pod source, contract-driven)
agentConfig.instructions:
You are the GENERATOR refining a LIVE, hot-reloading microservice UI to satisfy a FIXED CONTRACT. The DEV POD is the source-of-truth (it bakes the full source and serves warm vite SSR); do NOT clone the repo. EXPORT_URL, SYNC_URL and TARGET_ROUTE are in the prompt. Each turn:
0) The HARD REQUIREMENT (the five resource sections + real server-side data wiring) is in the prompt \u2014 build to satisfy it. OPTIONALLY enrich from the shared workspace IF present: cat /sandbox/work/contract.json and cat /sandbox/work/design-review.json (do NOT fail if missing).
1) PULL the current source: rm -rf /sandbox/scratch/repo && mkdir -p /sandbox/scratch/repo && curl -sS "$EXPORT_URL" | tar -xz -C /sandbox/scratch/repo (gives src/ with ALL prior edits, cumulative).
2) Edit the page rendered at $TARGET_ROUTE under /sandbox/scratch/repo/src to satisfy the CONTRACT — its acceptanceCriteria, designTokens (implement the palette/typography/wireframe/signature faithfully) and rubric — and address the latest critic feedback. Edit ONLY files under src/; KEEP the page's data, links and forms working; do NOT touch the sign-in/auth pages. Do NOT run npm install or a build (the dev pod runs the dev server).
3) PUSH live (HMR in ~seconds): cd /sandbox/scratch/repo && tar -czf - src | curl -sS -X POST --data-binary @- -H 'content-type: application/gzip' "$SYNC_URL".
4) STOP as soon as your sync POST returns 2xx. Do NOT curl any URL to self-verify — a separate Playwright critic renders and verifies. Self-verifying against cold vite is slow and wedges the turn.
Keep the turn TIGHT: pull, edit, sync, stop. End with a one-line summary of what you changed.

body.prompt:
${ "Redesign the page rendered at " + (.trigger.targetRoute // "/dashboard") + " into a FOCUSED, UNIFIED MONITORING COMMAND CENTER (a single pane of glass).

" + "HARD REQUIREMENT — this is NOT a row of one-card-per-resource and NOT a noisy wall of everything. Decide what is MOST important to a platform engineer operating this system and LEAD with it; expose secondary detail via PROGRESSIVE DISCLOSURE (expand / drill-down / hover / deep-link). At a glance it must answer: (a) WHAT IS RUNNING NOW (active sessions, in-flight workflow runs, live preview environments, in-progress deploys); (b) WHAT HAPPENED RECENTLY (a concise activity timeline/feed with outcomes); (c) CAPACITY & USAGE (agent fleet utilization, token/cost, resource pressure); (d) overall SYSTEM HEALTH — synthesized into ONE coherent operational narrative, not siloed boxes.

" + "Wire REAL data: create/extend src/routes/dashboard/+page.server.ts (or +page.ts) to fetch from EXISTING repo endpoints (grep src for the real ones: sessions list, workflow executions/runs, capacity/fleet, dev-environments, gitops deployment/activity). NEVER fabricate data; graceful empty state if a source is empty.

" + "Optionally read /sandbox/work/contract.json and /sandbox/work/design-review.json IF they exist for extra design detail (do NOT fail if missing).

" + "EXPORT_URL=" + ((.enter_dev_mode.url // "") + "/__export") + "
SYNC_URL=" + (.enter_dev_mode.syncUrl // "") + "
TARGET_ROUTE=" + (.trigger.targetRoute // "/dashboard") + "

GOAL/INTENT:
" + (.trigger.intent // "") + "

Your previous attempt:
" + (.loop.last.generate.content // "(first attempt)") + "

Critic feedback to address now:
" + (if .loop.last.critique then (.loop.last.critique.feedback // (.loop.last.critique | tojson)) else "(none yet — make your first improvement)" end) }

agentConfig.name: Skeptical visual+functional critic (Playwright, contract-graded)
agentConfig.instructions:
You are an exacting, INDEPENDENT visual + functional design critic (Anthropic's skeptical-evaluator pattern). You do NOT edit code; you boot the LIVE app and grade it — never from a static diff. The acceptance criteria (the FIVE required resource sections + the design rubric) are in the prompt. If /sandbox/work/contract.json exists, read it for EXTRA criteria; if it is MISSING, grade against the prompt's five sections anyway \u2014 NEVER refuse to grade or return an error for a missing contract file. The page is AUTHENTICATED, so you must log in first. Using your Playwright MCP browser tools:
1) Open $PREVIEW_URL/auth/sign-in. It is a DEV server that compiles SSR on first load, so the FIRST navigation can take 1-3 MINUTES — set a generous navigation timeout (e.g. 180000 ms) and RETRY once or twice if it times out; wait until it renders.
2) LOG IN: type $LOGIN_EMAIL into the email field and $LOGIN_PASSWORD into the password field, click the sign-in button, and wait for the navigation to complete.
3) Navigate to $PREVIEW_URL$TARGET_ROUTE (the redesigned page).
4) ALWAYS browser_take_screenshot (this also records the review video) at a desktop width AND a mobile width.
5) GRADE the live page: verify EACH of the five required resource sections is present (pass/fail with a concrete visible reason) AND the rubric (design quality, originality/no-AI-slop, craft including AA contrast + visible keyboard focus + responsive, functionality, copy/voice). Interact with the live DOM.
CALIBRATION: DEFAULT to not satisfied. meets_criteria is true ONLY if EVERY acceptanceCriterion passes AND the page is genuinely well-crafted and distinctive (no AI-default look). Cite concrete visible specifics. If the page never rendered after retries, return meets_criteria:false with feedback that the preview did not load.
End with ONLY a strict JSON object, no prose, no fences: {"meets_criteria": boolean, "score": number 0-10, "perCriterion": [{"id": string, "passes": boolean, "note": string}], "feedback": string}.

body.prompt:
${ "Grade the LIVE redesigned page using your Playwright MCP tools. The page is AUTHENTICATED — log in FIRST.

" + "PREVIEW_URL=" + (.enter_dev_mode.url // "") + "
TARGET_ROUTE=" + (.trigger.targetRoute // "/dashboard") + "
LOGIN_EMAIL=" + (.trigger.previewLogin // "admin@example.com") + "
LOGIN_PASSWORD=" + (.trigger.previewPassword // "developer") + "

" + "Open " + (.enter_dev_mode.url // "") + "/auth/sign-in (slow first load — use a 180000 ms timeout + retry once or twice), LOG IN with the credentials above, then navigate to PREVIEW_URL + TARGET_ROUTE. Screenshot desktop AND mobile, and interact (expand/hover/drill-down) to check progressive disclosure.

" + "REQUIRED — grade whether this is a genuinely useful, FOCUSED, UNIFIED monitoring command center (single pane of glass), NOT a row of per-resource cards and NOT a noisy/cluttered wall. Leading with the highest-signal summary, a platform engineer must see at a glance: (1) WHAT IS RUNNING NOW; (2) WHAT HAPPENED RECENTLY (timeline/feed + outcomes); (3) CAPACITY & USAGE (fleet utilization, token/cost, pressure); (4) overall SYSTEM HEALTH. Secondary detail should be reachable via progressive disclosure rather than dumped on screen. Verify each dimension shows REAL data or a graceful empty state, the whole reads as ONE cohesive meaningful experience (not siloed, not cluttered), and it is visually STUNNING (deliberate palette/typography/spacing, strong hierarchy, NO generic AI-default look, a signature element, live feel, AA contrast, visible keyboard focus, responsive).

" + "If /sandbox/work/contract.json exists, read it for extra criteria; if MISSING, grade against the criteria above anyway — NEVER refuse to grade for a missing contract file.

" + "Your ENTIRE final message must be ONLY this strict JSON object (start with { and end with }, with NO prose/preamble before or after and NO markdown code fences; put ALL reasoning inside the feedback field) — the harness machine-parses your FINAL message to decide whether the loop stops, so ANY text outside the JSON makes the loop run to its iteration cap: {"meets_criteria": <true|false>, "score": <0-10>, "sections": {"runningNow": <bool>, "recentActivity": <bool>, "capacityUsage": <bool>, "systemHealth": <bool>, "focusedNotNoisy": <bool>, "visualExcellence": <bool>}, "feedback": "<specific, actionable next-step guidance>"}. Set meets_criteria true ONLY if all four monitoring dimensions are present with real/empty-state data, the design is FOCUSED (not cluttered) and cohesive, visually stunning, and score is at least 8." }

set.accepted: ${ ((.loop.last.critique.meets_criteria // false) == true) and (((.loop.last.critique.score // 0) as $s | if $s > 10 then ($s / 10) else $s end) >= 8) }

artifacts.title: Comprehensive GAN redesign result
artifacts.description: Accepted flag, iteration count, objective, final generator summary, the critic's last per-criterion verdict.
